 {"id":"caam-zha","title":"Profile export/import for cross-machine transfer","description":"# Profile Export/Import for Cross-Machine Transfer

## What

Add \`caam export\` and \`caam import\` commands to easily transfer profiles between machines.

## Why - This is the UNIVERSAL headless solution

The most caam-native way to set up headless servers:

1. Login on machine with browser (laptop, workstation)
2. \`caam export codex/work > profile.tar.gz\`
3. \`scp profile.tar.gz user@server:~\`
4. On server: \`caam import profile.tar.gz\`
5. \`caam activate codex work\` - instant, no login needed!

This works for **ALL providers** (Codex, Claude, Gemini) because it transfers the auth FILES, not worrying about login mechanisms.

## Commands

### caam export

\`\`\`bash
# Export single profile
caam export codex/work > codex-work.tar.gz
caam export codex work -o codex-work.tar.gz

# Export all profiles for a tool
caam export codex --all > codex-all.tar.gz

# Export entire vault
caam export --all > full-vault.tar.gz
\`\`\`

### caam import

\`\`\`bash
# Import from file
caam import codex-work.tar.gz

# Import from stdin (for piping)
cat codex-work.tar.gz | caam import -

# Import with rename
caam import codex-work.tar.gz --as codex/server-work
\`\`\`

## Implementation

### Export format

Tar.gz containing:
- Profile directory structure from vault
- Metadata file with export timestamp, source machine, caam version
- Checksum for integrity

### File: cmd/caam/cmd/export.go

\`\`\`go
var exportCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "export <tool/profile>",
    Short: "Export profile(s) for transfer to another machine",
    Long: \`Export profile auth files for transfer to another machine.

This is the recommended way to set up profiles on headless servers:
1. Login normally on a machine with a browser
2. Export the profile: caam export codex/work > profile.tar.gz
3. Transfer to headless server: scp profile.tar.gz server:
4. Import on server: caam import profile.tar.gz
5. Activate: caam activate codex work

The exported file contains only the auth credentials, not session state.\`,
    RunE: runExport,
}
\`\`\`

## Security Considerations

- Exported files contain sensitive OAuth tokens
- Recommend encrypting with gpg for transfer: \`caam export ... | gpg -c > profile.tar.gz.gpg\`
- Add --encrypt flag for built-in encryption (nice-to-have)
- Document that exported tokens should be treated like passwords

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] \`caam export codex/work\` produces valid tar.gz
- [ ] \`caam import profile.tar.gz\` restores profile correctly
- [ ] Imported profile can be activated immediately
- [ ] Works for all providers (codex, claude, gemini)
- [ ] Handles name conflicts gracefully (--force or --as)

## Why This is Better Than Device Code

| Aspect | Device Code | Export/Import |
|--------|-------------|---------------|
| Works for Codex | ✅ | ✅ |
| Works for Claude | ❌ (buggy setup-token) | ✅ |
| Works for Gemini | ⚠️ (recent, untested) | ✅ |
| Leverages caam's core strength | ❌ | ✅ |
| One-time setup | Login each server | Login once, transfer |
| Implementation complexity | Medium | Low |","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T18:05:19.598114513-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T18:43:22.039830895-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T18:43:22.039830895-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented `caam export`/`caam import` for vault transfer (tar.gz) with manifest + sha256 checksums, conflict handling (--force, --as), atomic import via temp dir + rename, and unit tests. Added Vault.BasePath() accessor.","comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"caam-zha","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting work on export/import. Plan: add  +  commands (tar.gz), include metadata + checksum, handle stdin/stdout and name conflicts, add tests. Will keep it minimal + safe (tokens treated as secrets).","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:30:39Z"},{"id":5,"issue_id":"caam-zha","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting work on export/import. Plan: add `caam export` + `caam import` commands (tar.gz), include metadata + checksum, handle stdin/stdout and name conflicts, add tests. Tokens are secrets; docs will recommend encrypting tarballs.","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:30:53Z"},{"id":7,"issue_id":"caam-zha","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented export/import and landed to main. Usage: `caam export codex/work > profile.tar.gz` (or `-o file`), `caam export --all > full-vault.tar.gz`, then on target machine `caam import profile.tar.gz` (or `cat ... | caam import -`). Import verifies SHA256 checksums and is atomic per profile; supports `--force` and single-profile rename via `--as codex/server-work`.","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:44:56Z"}]}

-{"id":"caam-zidd","title":"Add TertiaryWindow and ModelWindows to UsageInfo","description":"# Task: Add TertiaryWindow and ModelWindows to UsageInfo

## Overview
Extend the UsageInfo struct in internal/usage/usage.go to support tertiary rate limit windows (e.g., Claude Opus-specific limits) and per-model breakdowns.

## Background
Claude Max has overlapping rate limits:
- Primary: 5-hour rolling window (all models)
- Secondary: 7-day weekly cap (all models)  
- Tertiary: Opus-specific daily/weekly limits (premium model)

Current UsageInfo only has Primary and Secondary.

## Technical Details

### Enhanced UsageInfo
```go
type UsageInfo struct {
    // Existing fields...
    PrimaryWindow   *UsageWindow
    SecondaryWindow *UsageWindow
    Credits         *CreditInfo
    
    // NEW fields
    TertiaryWindow  *UsageWindow              // Model-specific (e.g., Opus)
    ModelWindows    map[string]*UsageWindow   // Per-model breakdown
}
```

### Update AvailabilityScore
Modify AvailabilityScore() to include tertiary window in calculation.

### Update Claude Fetcher
Parse additional fields from Claude API response if available.

## Files to Modify
- internal/usage/usage.go
- internal/usage/claude.go
- internal/usage/usage_test.go","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:18:17.598922304-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T00:18:17.598922304-05:00"}

 {"id":"caam-zti","title":"Smart auto-backup before switch","description":"Prevent accidental data loss when switching profiles.

## The Problem
User modifies their auth (e.g., logs into new account in browser), then runs `caam activate foo`. Their unsaved changes are lost because the current state doesn't match any vault profile.

## Solution
Before restoring a profile, check if current auth state is 'unsaved':
1. Hash current auth files
2. Compare with all vault profiles
3. If NO MATCH → current state would be lost
4. Auto-backup to `_backup_<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>` profile
5. Continue with normal activate

## Config Option
```yaml
safety:
  auto_backup_before_switch: smart  # 'always' | 'smart' | 'never'
  max_auto_backups: 5  # Keep last N auto-backups, delete older
```

- 'smart': Only backup if state doesn't match any profile (default)
- 'always': Always backup before switch (creates many profiles)
- 'never': Skip (for power users who know what they're doing)

## Auto-backup Rotation
When max_auto_backups exceeded:
1. List all _backup_* profiles for the tool
2. Sort by timestamp (oldest first)
3. Delete oldest until count <= max

## Implementation
1. Add Vault.CurrentStateMatchesAny(fileSet) method
2. Add Vault.BackupCurrent(fileSet) -> '_backup_<timestamp>'
3. Add auto-backup rotation logic
4. Modify activate command to check config and call BackupCurrent
5. Print: 'Auto-backed up current state to _backup_20251217_143022'

## Files
- internal/authfile/authfile.go: New methods
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Add smart backup logic
- internal/config/spm_config.go: Add config options","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:44:52.070068986-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:31:15.970213702-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:31:15.970213702-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented smart auto-backup before switch. Adds BackupCurrent() for timestamped backups, RotateAutoBackups() for cleanup, and integrates with activate command based on safety config (always|smart|never modes).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-zti","depends_on_id":"caam-nh8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:45:04.238174857-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-zti","depends_on_id":"caam-3w5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:45:09.347228092-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":52,"issue_id":"caam-zti","author":"ubuntu","text":"[ubuntu at 2025-12-18]
FYI caam-nh8 is now closed: `_` prefix reserved for system vault profiles; delete requires --force; ls shows [system]; meta.json now includes {type, created_by}. Useful for _backup_* rotation work.","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:48:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-zxk","title":"Implement auto-sync triggering after auth changes","description":"# Task: Implement auto-sync triggering after auth changes

## Context
When auth is refreshed or backed up locally, automatically push the fresh token to all machines in the sync pool. This is the "push-based, not polling" requirement.

**IMPORTANT**: Auto-sync is OPT-IN. Users must explicitly enable it with `caam sync enable`.

## Trigger Points

### After Backup
When `caam backup <provider> <profile>` completes successfully:
1. Check if auto-sync is enabled
2. If yes, trigger sync for that specific profile

### After Token Refresh
When token is refreshed (via `caam refresh` or proactive refresh):
1. Check if auto-sync is enabled
2. If yes, trigger sync for that specific profile

### After Activate (when token changes)
When `caam activate` results in a token update:
1. Check if auto-sync is enabled
2. If yes, trigger sync

## Implementation

### Sync Hook Function
```go
package sync

// TriggerSyncIfEnabled checks if auto-sync is enabled and triggers sync
// Returns immediately - sync runs in background
func TriggerSyncIfEnabled(provider, profile string) {
    state, err := LoadSyncState()
    if err != nil {
        return // Sync not configured, silently ignore
    }
    
    // CRITICAL: Both must be enabled
    if !state.Pool.Enabled || !state.Pool.AutoSync {
        return // Sync disabled, do nothing
    }
    
    if len(state.Pool.Machines) == 0 {
        return // No machines to sync with
    }
    
    // Check throttle to prevent sync storms
    if !state.Throttler.ShouldSync(provider, profile) {
        return // Recently synced, skip
    }
    
    // Run sync in background goroutine
    go func() {
        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Minute)
        defer cancel()
        
        syncer := NewSyncer(state)
        results, err := syncer.SyncProfile(ctx, provider, profile)
        
        // Log results
        logSyncResults(results, err)
        
        // Queue failures for retry
        if err != nil || hasFailures(results) {
            state.Queue.Add(provider, profile, getFailedMachines(results))
            state.Save()
        }
        
        // Update throttle timestamp
        state.Throttler.RecordSync(provider, profile)
    }()
}
```

### Integration with Backup Command
In `cmd/caam/cmd/backup.go`:
```go
func runBackup(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // ... existing backup logic ...
    
    if err := vault.Backup(provider, profileName); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    fmt.Printf("Backed up %s auth to profile '%s'\n", provider, profileName)
    
    // Trigger sync if enabled (non-blocking)
    sync.TriggerSyncIfEnabled(provider, profileName)
    
    return nil
}
```

### Integration with Refresh Command
In `cmd/caam/cmd/refresh.go`:
```go
func runRefresh(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // ... existing refresh logic ...
    
    if err := refresher.Refresh(provider, profileName); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    fmt.Printf("Refreshed token for %s/%s\n", provider, profileName)
    
    // Trigger sync if enabled (non-blocking)
    sync.TriggerSyncIfEnabled(provider, profileName)
    
    return nil
}
```

### Sync Queue for Retries
When sync fails (machine offline, network error), queue for retry:

```go
type SyncQueue struct {
    Entries []QueueEntry `json:"entries"`
    MaxSize int          `json:"max_size"` // Default: 100
}

type QueueEntry struct {
    Provider    string    `json:"provider"`
    Profile     string    `json:"profile"`
    Machine     string    `json:"machine"`
    AddedAt     time.Time `json:"added_at"`
    Attempts    int       `json:"attempts"`
    LastAttempt time.Time `json:"last_attempt"`
    LastError   string    `json:"last_error"`
}

func (q *SyncQueue) Add(provider, profile string, failedMachines []string)
func (q *SyncQueue) ProcessPending(ctx context.Context) ([]*SyncResult, error)
func (q *SyncQueue) Clear()
func (q *SyncQueue) RemoveOld(maxAge time.Duration)
```

### Queue Processing
Process queue on `caam sync` or periodically during other operations:
```go
func processQueueIfNeeded(ctx context.Context) {
    state, err := LoadSyncState()
    if err != nil || !state.Pool.Enabled {
        return
    }
    
    // Clean up old entries (> 24 hours)
    state.Queue.RemoveOld(24 * time.Hour)
    
    if len(state.Queue.Entries) == 0 {
        return
    }
    
    // Process queue in background
    go func() {
        results, _ := state.Queue.ProcessPending(ctx)
        logSyncResults(results, nil)
    }()
}
```

### Rate Limiting (Throttling)
Prevent sync storms if many changes happen quickly:
```go
type SyncThrottler struct {
    lastSync    map[string]time.Time // key: "provider/profile"
    minInterval time.Duration        // Default: 30 seconds
    mu          sync.RWMutex
}

func NewThrottler(minInterval time.Duration) *SyncThrottler

func (t *SyncThrottler) ShouldSync(provider, profile string) bool {
    t.mu.RLock()
    defer t.mu.RUnlock()
    
    key := provider + "/" + profile
    if last, ok := t.lastSync[key]; ok {
        if time.Since(last) < t.minInterval {
            return false // Too soon, skip
        }
    }
    return true
}

func (t *SyncThrottler) RecordSync(provider, profile string) {
    t.mu.Lock()
    defer t.mu.Unlock()
    t.lastSync[provider+"/"+profile] = time.Now()
}
```

### Sync History Logging
```go
type SyncHistory struct {
    Entries   []SyncHistoryEntry `json:"entries"`
    MaxSize   int                `json:"max_size"` // Default: 1000
}

type SyncHistoryEntry struct {
    Timestamp time.Time         `json:"timestamp"`
    Trigger   string            `json:"trigger"` // "backup", "refresh", "manual", "retry"
    Provider  string            `json:"provider"`
    Profile   string            `json:"profile"`
    Machine   string            `json:"machine"`
    Action    string            `json:"action"`  // "push", "pull", "skip"
    Success   bool              `json:"success"`
    Error     string            `json:"error,omitempty"`
    Duration  time.Duration     `json:"duration"`
}

func (h *SyncHistory) Add(entry SyncHistoryEntry)
func (h *SyncHistory) Recent(limit int) []SyncHistoryEntry
func (h *SyncHistory) ForMachine(machineName string, limit int) []SyncHistoryEntry
func (h *SyncHistory) Errors(limit int) []SyncHistoryEntry
```

## Enabling Auto-Sync

### First Time Setup
```bash
$ caam sync enable

Auto-sync is now enabled. When you backup or refresh tokens,
they will automatically sync to machines in your sync pool.

Current sync pool: 0 machines
Add machines with: caam sync add <name> <address>

Tip: Use 'caam sync status' to check sync pool status.
```

### With Machines Already Added
```bash
$ caam sync enable

Auto-sync is now enabled.
Will sync with 3 machines: work-laptop, home-desktop, cloud-vm
```

### Disabling
```bash
$ caam sync disable

Auto-sync is now disabled.
Use 'caam sync' to manually sync when needed.
```

## Configuration
In `~/.caam/config.yaml`:
```yaml
sync:
  # Note: enabled and auto_sync are stored in sync state, not config
  # These are just defaults for new sync pools
  min_interval: 30s      # Minimum time between syncs for same profile
  retry_attempts: 3      # Max retry attempts for failed syncs
  retry_backoff: 5m      # Wait between retries
  queue_max_size: 100    # Max pending sync entries
  history_max_size: 1000 # Max history entries
```

## Status Feedback

### During Backup/Refresh (when auto-sync is enabled)
```bash
$ caam backup claude alice@gmail.com
Backed up claude auth to profile 'alice@gmail.com'
  Vault: /Users/jeff/.local/share/caam/vault/claude/alice@gmail.com
Syncing in background... (use 'caam sync status' to check)
```

### Sync Status Shows Pending
```bash
$ caam sync status
...
Background syncs: 1 in progress
Queue: 0 pending
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Auto-sync defaults to OFF (requires explicit enable)
- [ ] Sync triggers after backup command (when enabled)
- [ ] Sync triggers after refresh command (when enabled)
- [ ] Sync runs in background (non-blocking)
- [ ] Failed syncs are queued for retry
- [ ] Queue is processed on manual sync
- [ ] Old queue entries cleaned up (> 24h)
- [ ] Rate limiting prevents sync storms
- [ ] Sync history is logged
- [ ] Status feedback during backup/refresh
- [ ] `caam sync enable/disable` works correctly
- [ ] Config controls retry behavior
- [ ] Integration tests for trigger flow

## Files to Modify
- `cmd/caam/cmd/backup.go` - Add sync trigger
- `cmd/caam/cmd/refresh.go` - Add sync trigger
- `cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go` - Add sync trigger (optional)

## Files to Create
- `internal/sync/trigger.go` - Trigger logic
- `internal/sync/queue.go` - Queue management
- `internal/sync/throttle.go` - Rate limiting
- `internal/sync/history.go` - History logging

## Dependencies
- Depends on: caam-3wx (sync infrastructure)
- Depends on: caam-683 (SSH transport)
- Depends on: caam-npy (sync algorithm)
- Part of epic: caam-2bm (Multi-Machine Vault Sync)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:45:15.82848476-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:50:21.237116843-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:50:21.237116843-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented auto-sync triggering: autosync.go with TriggerSyncIfEnabled, SyncThrottler for rate limiting, queue processing helpers.
